marketState: neutral — bearish near-term technicals vs. bullish medium-term weather/positioning
summary: Front-month NG trades at $2.842, rated FAIR vs the model ($2.923, band $2.423-$3.423) but running well below the 16-year seasonal median (-9.7pp, -16.6% YTD vs -6.9% median) with low seasonal reliability this week. Near-term technicals are uniformly bearish across all six timeframes (negative MACD on most, 50<200 EMA cross on every horizon) and reinforced by a bearish storage surprise (+22 Bcf vs forecast, +180 Bcf vs 5y). The medium-term picture is different: 8-16 day demand is running +22.3% above the 5-year average near the top of its range, fair value drifts up toward $3.00 by day 21 while spot stays flat, and managed money is net short at just the 8th percentile of the past 5 years — a crowded, contrarian setup vulnerable to short-covering. Net view: lean short into near-term technical/storage weakness while positioning for a medium-term recovery driven by hot weather, rising fair value, and squeeze risk in positioning. Futures curve data is unavailable this run, which removes one confirming input for the short-term call.

longTerm.rationale: 8-16D demand is +22.3% vs 5y at the top of its range (rangePos 0.90) and fair value is drifting up from $2.923 now to $3.000 by +21D while spot stays flat, so the fair-value gap should close higher into peak cooling season. Managed money net short at the 8th 5y percentile (ratio 0.68) is a crowded, contrarian setup that historically unwinds via short-covering.
longTerm.invalidation: Seasonal reliability is Low (59pp 10-90% spread) and probHigher-in-4-weeks is only 38%; a deepening storage surplus (already +180 Bcf vs 5y) or weather normalizing back toward the 5y average in the 8-16D window would invalidate the bullish weather thesis.
